title: Plural forms
subtitle: How nouns change when there's more than one

hook.text: In English, plurals are simple — add -s, with a handful of exceptions (mouse→mice). Russian is more systematic, but the system has more patterns. Today you'll learn the three main plural endings and two important irregular plurals that appear constantly. Master these and you can pluralise almost any noun you encounter.

why.text: Plural formation in Russian depends on gender, the final consonant of the stem, and whether the spelling rules apply. There's a pattern to it — it's not random. Learning it as a set of rules (with the exceptions flagged) is faster than memorising each plural individually.

cards.title: Masculine plurals
cards.body: Most masculine nouns add -Ы or -И. The spelling rule: after Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч, write -И (not -Ы). Otherwise, use -Ы. Some masculine nouns take a stressed -А ending instead — these must be memorised.

cards.title: Feminine plurals
cards.body: Feminine nouns ending in -А drop the А and add -Ы or -И. After Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч, use -И. Feminines ending in -Я take -И. Feminines ending in -Ь take -И.

cards.title: Neuter plurals
cards.body: Neuter nouns ending in -О take -А. Neuter nouns ending in -Е take -Я.

cards.title: Supletive plurals — completely different words
cards.body: A few very common nouns form their plural from a different root entirely — called supletive plurals. These must be learned as separate vocabulary items. человек → люди (person → people) and ребёнок → дети (child → children) are the two you'll use constantly.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of книга?
questions.explanation: книга → книги. The А drops and -И replaces it (spelling rule: after Г, use И not Ы).

questions.prompt: What is the plural of слово?
questions.explanation: слово → слова. Neuter О-ending: drop О, add А.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of человек?
questions.explanation: человек → люди. Supletive plural — completely different root. Must be memorised.

cards.title: The spelling rules (7-letter rule)
cards.body: Russian has a crucial spelling rule: after Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч Ц, you cannot write Ы — use И instead. This applies in plural endings: урок→уроки (not уроки), книга→книги (not книги is actually correct here due to this rule). The 7-letter rule also applies in other contexts — learn it as a universal constraint.

cards.title: Irregular masculine -А plurals
cards.body: Some masculine nouns have irregular plurals ending in stressed -А. These are high-frequency words you'll use constantly. There's no grammatical rule to predict which nouns do this — memorise the list.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of стол?
questions.explanation: стол → столы. Masculine consonant ending: add -Ы.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of дверь?
What is the plural of ребёнок (child)?
questions.explanation: ребёнок → дети. Supletive plural — completely different root.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of окно?
questions.explanation: окно → окна. Neuter О-ending: О → А.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of урок?
questions.explanation: урок → уроки. Masculine + spelling rule: after К, use -И not -Ы.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of люди?
questions.explanation: люди is already the plural. The singular is человек. люди → людей in genitive plural.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of школа?

rules: Masculine: add -Ы (after hard consonants) or -И (after Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч). Exception: some take stressed -А (город→города)
Feminine -А/-Я: drop ending, add -Ы or -И. Feminine -Ь: drop Ь, add -И
Spelling rule: never write Ы after Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч — use И instead
summary.nextLesson: Next: numbers and nouns — why 2 дома but 5 домов, and the counting rules that govern Russian quantity.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of стол?
What is the plural of книга?
What is the plural of слово?
What is the plural of человек?
After which letters do you write -И instead of -Ы in plural?
options: After Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч
questions.answer: After Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч

questions.prompt: What is the plural of дом?
What is the plural of дверь?
What is the plural of ребёнок (child)?
